input.instructions:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B3-ambiguity-detection-pronoun-disambiguation
Title: B3. Ambiguity Detection (Pronoun Disambiguation)

Problem:
"The investigator wanted to interview the witness in person but he was too late. On his way, he got stuck in traffic."

Which option correctly explains the pronoun antecedent?

Answer choices: 5 options.

Grading target:
Exact match on the single letter.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B3-ambiguity-detection-pronoun-disambiguation
Title: B3. Ambiguity Detection (Pronoun Disambiguation)

Problem:
"The investigator wanted to interview the witness in person but he was too late. On his way, he got stuck in traffic."

Which option correctly explains the pronoun antecedent?

Answer choices: 5 options.

Grading target:
Exact match on the single letter.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

results.content:
The user wants a structured reformulation of a linguistic ambiguity detection task involving pronoun disambiguation.
node_root_helper_01 (transmuted_child): Task: Perform pronoun disambiguation and ambiguity detection for the provided text. Text: The investigator wanted to interview the witness in person but he was too late. On his way, he got stuck in traffic. Entities: 1. The investigator (Subject/Agent), 2. The witness (Object/Patient). Linguistic Elements: The pronouns he (used twice) and his (used once). Objective: Identify which entity (the investigator or the witness) serves as the antecedent for the pronouns he and his, and determine if the sentence structure creates ambiguity or if a specific antecedent can be logically/grammatically deduced. The goal is to select the correct option from the five provided choices that accurately explains the antecedent relationship. Note: The final output must provide the single letter corresponding to the correct option and a brief justification.
